title: Build a Social Media Platform
description: Create a full-stack social platform with user authentication, profiles, a news feed with infinite scroll, likes, comments, follows, and real-time notifications using React, Flask, and WebSockets.
keyPoints: Design a social-graph database schema with users, posts, follows, likes, and comments
Implement JWT authentication with refresh tokens
Build a news feed with infinite scroll pagination
Create a like and comment system with optimistic updates

blocks.heading: Architecture Overview
blocks.body: This project uses a React single-page app on the front end and a Flask REST API on the back end. Data lives in PostgreSQL, and real-time notifications flow through Flask-SocketIO. We will build the core features step by step: auth, profiles, posts, feed, interactions, and notifications.

cards.title: JWT Auth
cards.body: Register, login, and protected routes with access and refresh tokens.

cards.title: User Profiles
cards.body: Avatar upload, bio, and public profile pages.

cards.title: Post Creation
cards.body: Rich text posts with image attachments.

cards.title: News Feed
cards.body: Chronological feed from followed users with infinite scroll.

cards.title: Likes & Comments
cards.body: Nested comment threads and like toggles with counts.

cards.title: Notifications
cards.body: Real-time alerts when someone likes, comments, or follows.

blocks.heading: Database Schema
blocks.body: The social graph is modeled with five core tables. The follows table is a many-to-many self-join on users. Likes and comments both reference the posts table.

blocks.description: SQL to create all five tables with indexes for common queries.

blocks.heading: Backend: Flask API
blocks.body: The API is organized into blueprints: auth, users, posts, and notifications. We use Flask-JWT-Extended for authentication and Flask-SocketIO for real-time events.

blocks.title: Project Setup and Dependencies
blocks.body: Create a virtual environment and install the required packages.
blocks.code:
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install flask flask-cors flask-jwt-extended flask-socketio psycopg2-binary werkzeug

blocks.description: Flask application factory with JWT, CORS, and SocketIO initialization.
blocks.code:
from flask import Flask
from flask_cors import CORS
from flask_jwt_extended import JWTManager
from flask_socketio import SocketIO
import os

socketio = SocketIO(cors_allowed_origins="*")
jwt = JWTManager()

def create_app():
    app = Flask(__name__)
    app.config["SECRET_KEY"] = os.getenv("SECRET_KEY", "dev-secret")
    app.config["JWT_SECRET_KEY"] = os.getenv("JWT_SECRET", "jwt-dev-secret")
    app.config["JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRES"] = 900   # 15 minutes
    app.config["JWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRES"] = 86400 * 30  # 30 days

    CORS(app)
    jwt.init_app(app)
    socketio.init_app(app)

    from routes.auth import auth_bp
    from routes.users import users_bp
    from routes.posts import posts_bp

    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp, url_prefix="/api/auth")
    app.register_blueprint(users_bp, url_prefix="/api/users")
    app.register_blueprint(posts_bp, url_prefix="/api/posts")

    return app

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = create_app()
    socketio.run(app, debug=True, port=5000)

blocks.description: Register and login endpoints that return JWT access and refresh tokens.
Post CRUD, feed endpoint with cursor-based pagination, like toggle, and comment creation.
blocks.code:
from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify
from flask_jwt_extended import jwt_required, get_jwt_identity
from db import get_db
from app import socketio

posts_bp = Blueprint("posts", __name__)

@posts_bp.route("", methods=["POST"])
@jwt_required()
def create_post():
    user_id = int(get_jwt_identity())
    data = request.get_json()
    body = data.get("body", "").strip()
    image_url = data.get("image_url")
    if not body:
        return jsonify(error="Post body required"), 400

    db = get_db()
    cur = db.cursor()
    cur.execute(
        "INSERT INTO posts (author_id, body, image_url) VALUES (%s, %s, %s) RETURNING id, created_at",
        (user_id, body, image_url),
    )
    post_id, created_at = cur.fetchone()
    db.commit()
    return jsonify(id=post_id, author_id=user_id, body=body, image_url=image_url,
                   created_at=created_at.isoformat()), 201

@posts_bp.route("/feed", methods=["GET"])
@jwt_required()
def feed():
    """Return posts from users the current user follows, newest first.
    Uses cursor-based pagination via ?before=<post_id>&limit=N."""
    user_id = int(get_jwt_identity())
    before = request.args.get("before", type=int)
    limit = min(request.args.get("limit", 20, type=int), 50)

    db = get_db()
    cur = db.cursor()
    query = """
        SELECT p.id, p.author_id, u.username, u.avatar_url,
               p.body, p.image_url, p.created_at,
               COUNT(DISTINCT l.user_id) AS like_count,
               COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS comment_count,
               EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM likes WHERE user_id=%s AND post_id=p.id) AS liked
        FROM posts p
        JOIN follows f ON f.following_id = p.author_id AND f.follower_id = %s
        JOIN users u ON u.id = p.author_id
        LEFT JOIN likes l ON l.post_id = p.id
        LEFT JOIN comments c ON c.post_id = p.id
    """
    params = [user_id, user_id]
    if before:
        query += " WHERE p.id < %s"
        params.append(before)
    query += " GROUP BY p.id, u.username, u.avatar_url ORDER BY p.created_at DESC LIMIT %s"
    params.append(limit)

    cur.execute(query, params)
    columns = [d[0] for d in cur.description]
    posts = [dict(zip(columns, row)) for row in cur.fetchall()]
    for p in posts:
        p["created_at"] = p["created_at"].isoformat()
    return jsonify(posts=posts, has_more=len(posts) == limit)

@posts_bp.route("/<int:post_id>/like", methods=["POST"])
@jwt_required()
def toggle_like(post_id):
    user_id = int(get_jwt_identity())
    db = get_db()
    cur = db.cursor()
    cur.execute("SELECT 1 FROM likes WHERE user_id=%s AND post_id=%s", (user_id, post_id))
    if cur.fetchone():
        cur.execute("DELETE FROM likes WHERE user_id=%s AND post_id=%s", (user_id, post_id))
        liked = False
    else:
        cur.execute("INSERT INTO likes (user_id, post_id) VALUES (%s, %s)", (user_id, post_id))
        liked = True
        # Notify post author
        cur.execute("SELECT author_id FROM posts WHERE id=%s", (post_id,))
        author_id = cur.fetchone()[0]
        if author_id != user_id:
            socketio.emit("notification", {
                "type": "like", "post_id": post_id, "from_user_id": user_id
            }, room=f"user_{author_id}")
    db.commit()
    cur.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM likes WHERE post_id=%s", (post_id,))
    count = cur.fetchone()[0]
    return jsonify(liked=liked, like_count=count)

@posts_bp.route("/<int:post_id>/comments", methods=["POST"])
@jwt_required()
def add_comment(post_id):
    user_id = int(get_jwt_identity())
    data = request.get_json()
    body = data.get("body", "").strip()
    parent_id = data.get("parent_id")
    if not body:
        return jsonify(error="Comment body required"), 400

    db = get_db()
    cur = db.cursor()
    cur.execute(
        "INSERT INTO comments (post_id, author_id, parent_id, body) VALUES (%s,%s,%s,%s) RETURNING id, created_at",
        (post_id, user_id, parent_id, body),
    )
    comment_id, created_at = cur.fetchone()
    db.commit()
    return jsonify(id=comment_id, post_id=post_id, author_id=user_id,
                   body=body, parent_id=parent_id, created_at=created_at.isoformat()), 201

from flask import Blueprint, jsonify
from flask_jwt_extended import jwt_required, get_jwt_identity
from db import get_db

users_bp = Blueprint("users", __name__)

@users_bp.route("/<username>", methods=["GET"])
def get_profile(username):
    db = get_db()
    cur = db.cursor()
    cur.execute("""
        SELECT u.id, u.username, u.display_name, u.bio, u.avatar_url, u.created_at,
               (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM follows WHERE following_id = u.id) AS followers,
               (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM follows WHERE follower_id = u.id) AS following
        FROM users u WHERE u.username = %s
    """, (username,))
    row = cur.fetchone()
    if not row:
        return jsonify(error="User not found"), 404
    cols = [d[0] for d in cur.description]
    profile = dict(zip(cols, row))
    profile["created_at"] = profile["created_at"].isoformat()
    return jsonify(profile)

@users_bp.route("/<int:target_id>/follow", methods=["POST"])
@jwt_required()
def toggle_follow(target_id):
    user_id = int(get_jwt_identity())
    if user_id == target_id:
        return jsonify(error="Cannot follow yourself"), 400
    db = get_db()
    cur = db.cursor()
    cur.execute("SELECT 1 FROM follows WHERE follower_id=%s AND following_id=%s", (user_id, target_id))
    if cur.fetchone():
        cur.execute("DELETE FROM follows WHERE follower_id=%s AND following_id=%s", (user_id, target_id))
        following = False
    else:
        cur.execute("INSERT INTO follows (follower_id, following_id) VALUES (%s, %s)", (user_id, target_id))
        following = True
    db.commit()
    cur.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM follows WHERE following_id=%s", (target_id,))
    count = cur.fetchone()[0]
    return jsonify(following=following, follower_count=count)

blocks.heading: Frontend: React Components
blocks.body: The React app uses React Router for navigation, a global auth context for JWT management, and react-query for server state. Below are the key components.

blocks.description: Auth context that stores tokens and provides login/logout helpers.
A single post in the feed with like button, comment count, and author info.
Infinite-scroll feed using Intersection Observer and cursor-based pagination.
Recursive comment thread supporting nested replies.

blocks.heading: Real-Time Notifications
blocks.body: We use Flask-SocketIO on the backend and socket.io-client on the frontend. Each user joins a personal room so they only receive their own notifications.

blocks.description: WebSocket event handlers for joining rooms and emitting notifications.
React hook that connects to the WebSocket and listens for notification events.
Axios instance with automatic JWT header injection and token refresh interceptor.

blocks.title: Security Considerations
blocks.body: In production, store refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies instead of localStorage to prevent XSS attacks. Use HTTPS everywhere, rate-limit auth endpoints, and validate all user input on the server side.

blocks.heading: Next Steps
items: Add image upload with pre-signed S3 URLs for post and avatar images
Implement full-text search for users and posts using PostgreSQL tsvector
Add hashtag parsing and trending topics
Build a direct messaging feature with WebSocket rooms

blocks.heading: Summary
blocks.body: You have built the core of a social platform: JWT authentication, user profiles, a cursor-paginated news feed with infinite scroll, likes with optimistic UI updates, nested comments, follow/unfollow, and real-time WebSocket notifications. This architecture scales well and covers the fundamental patterns you will find in production social applications.
